description: Complex educational landing page about Ralph Loop, implemented incrementally by Ralph in static HTML/CSS/JS with deliberate layout, interaction, and accessibility.

userStories.title: Build an intentional editorial hero and narrative structure
userStories.description: As a technical reader, I want a non-generic hero and structured narrative sections so I can immediately understand the Ralph Loop and why it is different from toy agent demos.
acceptanceCriteria: The page includes a high-impact hero with title, subtitle, context callout, and a clearly visible CTA.
The layout uses at least three distinct narrative zones (hero, architecture story, execution story) with intentional visual rhythm.
The page avoids generic boilerplate styling and uses custom CSS variables, spacing scale, and typography hierarchy.
The implementation updates src/features/demo/index.html and src/features/demo/styles.css.
userStories.notes: Keep HTML/CSS/JS only. No frameworks. Preserve educational tone and avoid hype claims.

userStories.title: Add interactive macro-loop and micro-loop visualizations
userStories.description: As an engineer validating the process, I want interactive visualizations for macro-loop and micro-loop so I can inspect each phase and understand operational sequencing.
acceptanceCriteria: The page contains separate macro-loop and micro-loop sections with clear phase lists.
Each loop has interactive controls (tabs, toggles, or buttons) that change the visible explanation.
The interaction is progressively enhanced: content remains understandable with JavaScript disabled.
The implementation updates src/features/demo/index.html, src/features/demo/styles.css, and src/features/demo/script.js.
userStories.notes: Prefer simple robust JS over animation-heavy code.

userStories.title: Show dogfooding evidence panel and execution checklist
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a panel that explains verification gates and dogfooding evidence so I can trust the process output.
acceptanceCriteria: The page includes an explicit evidence panel describing TDD, CLI-first, peer review, and learnings promotion gates.
The page includes a checklist/timeline style component for story progression states.
Visuals include meaningful states (pending, implementing, review, passed, blocked) without relying only on color.
The implementation updates src/features/demo/index.html, src/features/demo/styles.css, and src/features/demo/script.js.
userStories.notes: Make this section audit-friendly and easy to scan.

userStories.title: Polish responsiveness and keyboard accessibility
userStories.description: As a user on desktop or mobile, I want responsive and accessible behavior so the page remains useful across screen sizes and input methods.
acceptanceCriteria: The page adapts layout between mobile and desktop with deliberate breakpoints.
All interactive elements are keyboard operable with visible focus states.
Semantic headings and landmark structure are clear for assistive technologies.
The implementation updates src/features/demo/index.html and src/features/demo/styles.css, and updates src/features/demo/script.js only if needed.
userStories.notes: Do not overcomplicate; focus on practical accessibility wins.
